message:
Here's a hard truth from the exam room: one of the most common health problems I see is also one of the most preventable — extra weight. 🐶

It's easy to miss, because it creeps on slowly and those begging eyes are hard to resist. But even a few extra pounds put real strain on a dog's joints and raise the risk of arthritis, diabetes, and heart trouble. Try this quick at-home check: run your hands along your dog's sides — you should be able to feel their ribs without pressing hard, and from above you should see a visible "waist" tucking in behind the ribs. No waist, no ribs? It's worth a conversation with your vet.

The fix usually isn't dramatic — measuring meals instead of free-feeding, swapping high-calorie treats for veggies like green beans or carrot sticks, and adding a little more daily movement. Small, steady changes add up. 💛

How do you keep your pup at a healthy weight — any go-to low-calorie treats? Share them below! 🐾
